_description: AC-6 parity fixture (MC sanity veto control): the position arms on tick 0 (mc=10 in [5,15)), then the return drops deep below the HWM-anchored stop for many ticks — BUT mc is held at 70, at/above MC_SANITY_THRESHOLD (60). compute_exit_confirmation's MC sanity gate vetoes the trailing-stop exit ('if we still think we beat the benchmark, don't capitulate'), so NO exit fires on either path. This is a control fixture: it confirms the production harness and the replay agree on the veto, and that the replay honours the MC sanity gate. The open-coded pre-fix replay used the same literal 60.0, so it also vetoes — this fixture must stay parity-green pre AND post fix, proving the parity harness does not emit false REDs.
_provenance: Hand-derived input sequence. Schema = synthetic_history tick dict. The parity test asserts production and the replay produce an identical all-None exit-decision sequence (the veto holds for the whole session).
